[164:18]they will be swayed by advertisements, by endorsements,

[173:34]released the total number of deportations for a fiscal year?

[174:01]for fiscal year 2025.

[174:10]was 442,000.

[179:00]So, what does this mean?

[179:02]Well, during the administration of Barack Obama, you had two categories of what counts

[179:08]as a deportation, removals and returns.

[179:14]An interior removal is when ICE, Immigration Customs Enforcement, goes to, let's say a

[179:22]Home Depot parking lot in Chicago, Illinois, or they go to a farm in Iowa, they go to a

[179:29]meat processing plant in Nebraska, and ICE will go there.

[179:34]They will find some illegal alien who has no papers, no citizenship, no residency.

[179:44]Detain that person and remove that person from the country.

[179:49]That is one kind of deportation, that is an interior removal.

[179:53]And when we think of deportations, obviously that is what we're thinking.

[179:58]What is catch and release at the border it's when an illegal alien shows up at the border

[180:03]We catch them detain them and then we release them into the interior of the country and then we lose track of them

[180:09]and you've got millions and millions of illegal aliens that have either jumped the border or

[180:15]They're a catch and release or they overstated visa

[180:19]They slipped in between the cracks, whatever and now we have these millions and millions

[180:24]and millions of people living inside our borders, inside the interior, inside every

[180:29]major city and they've got no residency, no citizenship, no paperwork, and they have

[180:36]no legal standing to be here.

[180:38]And obviously that is the problem and we talk about deportations, we're talking about rounding

[180:46]We shouldn't have all these illegal aliens in inside the country.

[180:50]They should be picked up and kicked out.

[180:52]That's an interior removal.

[180:54]But there's another category that is classified under DHS and their statistics as a deportation.

[181:03]It is called a border return.

[181:06]And a return is when an illegal alien, which in this case would actually really just be a foreign national.

[181:12]Let's say you live in Guatemala, you live in Honduras.

[181:17]You make the journey up through Mexico, you arrive at our southern border, and at that

[181:24]point you're a foreign national, you're a citizen of Guatemala, you're a citizen of

[181:28]Honduras, you emigrated, you surrender at a port of entry at the U.S. border, border patrol

[181:35]rather than ICE will pick you up and turn you away.

[181:39]You show up, either you're caught jumping the fence,

[181:42]or you surrender at a port of entry.

[181:44]Either way, Border Patrol apprehends you

[181:47]and they send you back to where you came from.

[181:49]They pick you up and they say,

[181:52]And this happens immediately.

[181:57]Border Patrol picks them up and sends them back.

[182:02]Interior removal, return at the border.

[182:05]One is enforced by ICE inside the interior

[182:09]The other is enforced by Border Patrol

[182:12]and it happens at the border.

[182:13]And we would just call that border enforcement.

[182:15]That's just having a border.

[194:00]We're going to include all these stipulations at hamstring ICE operations.

[194:04]And if Republicans in the Senate don't agree to pass it, if you don't agree to sign on

[194:09]to the legislation, well, then we're going to defund the government.

[194:17]And in the end, the White House is gonna walk away,

[194:20]they're going to agree to all these stipulations.

[194:22]And why are the Republicans going to be into that?

[194:26]I'm gonna tell you why,

[194:27]because the Democrats will be doing the Republicans a favor.

[194:32]Republicans do not want mass deportations for two reasons.

[194:38]If you have millions and millions of illegal aliens

[194:41]being deported,

[194:42]then you're going to have a massive increase in labor cost.

[194:47]You're going to have a big depression in demand, in aggregate demand.

[194:55]Let's say you deport three million illegal aliens.

[194:58]You know what illegal aliens do?

[195:00]They buy stuff.

[195:02]They buy cars.

[195:03]They buy fuel.

[195:04]They buy food, they take vacations.

[195:07]They buy everything that everybody else buys.

[195:09]They have Netflix subscriptions.

[195:11]They buy stuff.

[195:13]They have demand for housing and rental units.

[195:16]If you deport three million people,

[195:18]that's three million fewer consumers.

[195:21]And so you have less demand.

[195:25]But those illegal aliens also have jobs

[195:27]and they do them for cheap and they do them under the table.

[195:30]They do all the landscaping.

[195:32]They are the maids.

[195:34]They do all of the trade work. So they are the electricians. They are the carpenters. They are the painters

[195:42]And by the way, that is kind of what supports the entire economy, the municipal and local

[195:48]governments.

[195:49]They need contractors, they need inspectors, they need these kinds of people.

[195:53]Every business, every private sector, commercial business and government, they're hiring

[195:58]landscapers, they're hiring maids and cleaners and janitors.

[196:02]Where are they going to find all these replacements?

[196:04]Well, they're going to need to pay Americans a living wage.

[196:08]And they're going to need to provide healthcare and they're going to need to comply with OSHA and they're going to need to pay payroll taxes and all the rest of it.

[196:16]So what is this going to do? Well, it's going to eat into profits and it's going to eat into aggregate demand.

[196:23]It's going to eat into consumer spending. And what all this points to is a slowdown in economic growth, lack of profits for investment, lack of consumption.

[196:35]which is growth, that's how GDP is calculated.

[196:43]because they don't wanna pay a labor force more.

[196:45]The municipal government doesn't wanna pay more for,

[196:48]I mean, they need these people.

[196:55]any kind of appreciating assets in this country,

[196:57]they don't wanna see that happen.

[196:58]Anybody that owns real estate, any landlord,

[197:01]they don't wanna see a depression

[197:03]in the demand for rental units.

[197:11]and the rest of them, they don't want to get rid of all these consumers and producers.

[197:34]they're underwater with Hispanic males and they know that's going to be a big problem

[197:39]for them even in 2026.

[197:47]You're not getting them this year.

[197:49]And if you think that all we need to do is vote for them one more time and then we're

[197:55]going to surely get them next year or in 2028, let me just spoil it for you.

[198:01]You're not going to get them next year or the year after that either.

[199:42]They don't have the infrastructure.
